name: Kate Orff
practice: Landscape as infrastructure, oyster-tecture, ecological urbanism
approach: Landscape is not decoration applied after the building — it IS the infrastructure. Oyster reefs as wave attenuation. Constructed wetlands as water treatment. Living systems that perform engineering functions while providing habitat and beauty.
body_of_work: SCAPE Studio founder. Living Breakwaters (Staten Island) — oyster reefs as coastal defense. Lexington Community Farm. Toward an Urban Ecology. MacArthur Fellow 2017.
why_instructive: She merges sphere layers 2 (infrastructure) and 3 (environmental) — living systems that do engineering work. This is what sphere activation looks like when it's both beautiful and functional.

dome_relevance.9: Environment — ecological systems that clean and protect

sphere_relevance.7: Activation — ecological infrastructure as public experience
sphere_relevance.8: Permanence — living systems that strengthen over time
sphere_relevance.10: Catalyst — one living infrastructure project inspires others
